summary_one_line: Profile data-colorspace FOURCC at offset 0x10 must be a non-RGB signature so the cmsSigRgbData switch arm falls through.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The 4-byte data-colorspace signature at offset 0x10-0x13 of the ICC header must be a non-RGB FOURCC (e.g. 'Lab ', 'CMY ', 'MCH1', 'MCH4', 'XYZ') rather than 'RGB ' (52 47 42 20), so _cmsEndPointsBySpace's switch on Space does not enter the cmsSigRgbData case and instead reaches the Lab/CMYK/CMY/default arms, taking the FALSE (non-RGB) side of the blocker at cmspcs.c:732.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry varied non-RGB colorspace tags at offset 0x10-0x13: 'CMY ', 'Lab ', 'MCH1', 'MCH4' (W bytes at 0x10 are 4c/43/4d/35 = L/C/M/5, never the loser's uniform 52='R'), so the dispatch lands on a later case arm and exits the RGB arm via its false side.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Every loser-blocking seed has the colorspace field fixed to 'RGB ' (offset 0x10-0x13 = 52 47 42 20, 20/20 occurrences), so Space == cmsSigRgbData always holds, the RGB switch arm is entered (true side), and the blocked false side at cmspcs.c:732 is never taken.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc intercept the integer/string CMP that dispatches the switch on the colorspace signature, log the runtime operand (the cmsSig* FOURCC constants such as cmsSigLabData/cmsSigCmykData) into the CmpLogObserver buffer, and I2SRandReplace substitutes those logged constants into the input at the offset where the current 'RGB ' tag appears. naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and must blindly mutate a 4-byte field into a valid alternate FOURCC, which never happens in 10 trials.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked side is the false branch of the cmsSigRgbData switch arm in _cmsEndPointsBySpace.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: Source line:        case cmsSigRgbData:  if (White)    *White = RGBwhite;

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked side is the false (non-RGB) side of this branch.
The switch dispatches on Space, and winner-only coverage reaches the Lab/CMYK/CMY arms while RGB is hit by both.
Loser inputs all carry the 'RGB ' colorspace tag at offset 0x10-0x13.
Winner inputs carry varied non-RGB colorspace tags (CMY/Lab/MCH) at the same offsets.
Loser-blocking seeds carry 'RGB XYZ' at offset 0x10.
Both decisive pairs differ from their loser by exactly the I2S technique.
cmplog/vpc add I2SRandReplace which substitutes a logged CMP operand at matching input offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive and value_profile have no I2SRandReplace mutator.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 2234 --role W --fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --trial 1 --seed 072456d8257084f6` and observed 1 I2S-floor dash row (mutation_op = -) at depth 13 of the 14-level ancestor chain, which under the current build is lower-bound evidence of an I2SRandReplace find in this vpc winner's lineage.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose only gate is a 4-byte colorspace-FOURCC equality switch where cmplog/vpc fail to reach the non-RGB arms (i.e. I2S provides no advantage over naive/value_profile in reaching the false side).

weakest_evidence_point: The exact runtime CMP that dispatches the colorspace switch is inferred from source rather than confirmed in the CmpLogObserver buffer; the logged operand could be the parsed enum rather than the raw FOURCC bytes, which would weaken the byte-offset substitution story.
